teachingPoint: Platform SDK abstraction via fabric interface — services NEVER import Canva SDK directly. All Canva element operations go through ICanvaFabric interface. Factory resolves provider at runtime based on FREEDOM config.
positiveExample: @Inject(CANVA_FABRIC) canva: ICanvaFabric; T650 calls canva.getTextElement(elementId) — fabric provider handles SDK, authentication, and error translation.
negativeExample: import { CanvaClient } from '@canva/sdk'; const element = canvaClient.getText(elementId); — Direct SDK import violates Fabric First (Rule 1). Provider swap impossible.
discriminatingConstraint: T650 depends only on ICanvaFabric interface. No SDK imports in service code. Factory creates CanvaSDKProvider | CanvaRestApiProvider at startup based on config.

teachingPoint: Element type mapping establishes canonical transformation from Canva text element types (TEXT, HEADING, BODY, CAPTION) to XIIGen normalized schema (elementType: 'text'). Mapping stored in FREEDOM config, not hardcoded.
positiveExample: FREEDOM config: { canva: { textMappings: { TEXT: 'text', HEADING: 'heading-1', BODY: 'body-text' } } }. T650 reads mapping at ORDER 1, validates input type exists in mapping, transforms to canonical name.
negativeExample: Hardcoded switch (canvaType) { case 'TEXT': return 'text'; default: throw Error() } — Impossible to change mapping without redeployment. No fallback for unknown types.
discriminatingConstraint: Element type mapping from FREEDOM config only. ORDER 1 validation that input type exists in mapping. Unknown types return ElementMappingNotFound before any write.

teachingPoint: Coordinate system translation: Canva uses absolute pixel coordinates in canvas units; XIIGen normalizes to percentage-based layout (0-100 for width/height, relative to container). Translation formula: (pixelValue / canvasWidth) * 100.
positiveExample: T650 reads canvasWidth from element.parent.width, then: normalizedX = (element.x / canvasWidth) * 100. Stores normalized coordinates in xiigen-element-transform index for layout independence.
negativeExample: Storing raw pixel coordinates directly. Layout breaks if canvas is resized. No portable representation across different export targets (PDF, HTML, Figma).
discriminatingConstraint: Coordinate transformation at ORDER 1 before storage. Canvas dimensions validated (> 0). Normalized values 0-100 stored in transform record. Inverse transformation available for re-export.

teachingPoint: Style property conversion: Canva font properties (fontFamily, fontSize, fontWeight, color) map to XIIGen normalized style schema. Color stored as hex code; fontSize normalized to pt units; fontWeight mapped to numeric scale (100-900).
positiveExample: T650 reads Canva style: { font: 'Helvetica', size: '24px', weight: 'bold', fill: 'rgb(255,0,0)' }. Transforms to { fontFamily: 'Helvetica', fontSize: 24, fontWeight: 700, color: '#FF0000' }. Stored in style transformation record.
negativeExample: Storing style as-is (mixed units: px, em, named colors). Style breaks on export to HTML (needs pt), PDF (needs pt), or web (needs mixed). No normalized pivot point.
discriminatingConstraint: Style normalization at ORDER 2 (after type validation). All color values converted to #RRGGBB hex. Font size normalized to numeric pt units. Font weight mapped to CSS numeric scale (100-900).

description: Platform SDK abstraction via ICanvaFabric interface. Services depend only on fabric interface, never SDK directly. Factory resolves provider at runtime based on FREEDOM config. Enables provider swap (SDK → REST API) without code change.

Element type mapping establishes canonical transformation from platform-specific types to XIIGen normalized schema. Mapping stored in FREEDOM config for runtime flexibility. Validation ensures unknown types fail fast at ORDER 1.

Coordinate system translation from pixel-based (platform-specific) to percentage-based layout (0-100). Formula: (pixelValue / canvasDimension) * 100. Enables portable export across formats without layout distortion.

Style property conversion to normalized schema: color → hex (#RRGGBB), fontSize → pt units, fontWeight → CSS numeric scale (100-900). Unified pivot point for multi-format export (HTML, PDF, web, Figma).
